test: Minimum detectable effect (MDE) for the DELTA interaction slope-shift at tau=0.01 (leverage-interaction MDE).
what_this_does: Converts the shock x oi_high interaction non-rejection into a detectability statement: the smallest leverage-cycle amplification (delta-slope-shift) the canonical bootstrap could have detected (MDE_delta@80), set as a ratio against the direct tail effect |beta_direct| it would have to amplify.
derived_not_reestimated: CIs read from canonical quantile_interaction_bootstrap_fast.csv; direct effects read from canonical quantile_lp_results.csv. SE inferred from each CI half-width / z_{0.975} so MDE is exactly consistent with the published intervals. NOTHING is re-estimated; the locked QLP equation and the canonical bootstrap are untouched.

object.delta_interaction: beta_interaction (the change in the shock's tail effect when oi_high flips on) at tau=0.01, horizons [0, 3, 6, 12, 24]; CIs from quantile_interaction_bootstrap_fast.csv.
object.beta_direct: |beta_shock| at tau=0.01 from quantile_lp_results.csv — the direct tail effect the interaction would have to amplify.

formulas.mde_50_meaning: power=0.50 => z_power=0 => MDE = z_{1-alpha/2}*SE = CI half-width (smallest 'just-significant' delta-slope-shift).
formulas.machinery_source: Identical se_from_ci -> MDE machinery as run_mde_equivalence.py (mde_equivalence_meta.json).

identification_guardrail.delta_is_not_asymmetry: delta is a REGIME-SLOPE-SHIFT (effect change when oi_high turns on), NOT a down-minus-up difference. It does NOT inherit the sigma-cancellation that narrows the asymmetry object's CI — that absence of cancellation is WHY the delta CI is wide.
identification_guardrail.no_tost: There is NO pre-registered delta-SESOI in interaction-slope units, so NO TOST / equivalence test is run against the asymmetry SESOI. MDE is reported ONLY. The bounded-negligible reasoning of the asymmetry/MDE-equivalence object is NOT imported.

pre_planned.UNDERPOWERED-NULL: CONFIRMATORY: MDE_delta@80 materially > |beta_direct| (ratio > 1) => detectability (underpowered) null; supports the manuscript's leverage-interaction prose.
pre_planned.TIGHT-NULL: NON-CONFIRMATORY: MDE_delta@80 <= |beta_direct| (ratio <= 1) => genuine tight null; the leverage-interaction prose must FLIP to bounded-negligible language. SURFACED if it fires.

per_horizon.reading: MDE_delta@80=0.1088 is 3.38x the direct tail effect |beta_direct|=0.03217: the design could only have detected an amplification larger than the direct effect, so the interaction non-rejection is a DETECTABILITY (underpowered) null.

MDE_delta@80=0.2713 is 2.58x the direct tail effect |beta_direct|=0.1051: the design could only have detected an amplification larger than the direct effect, so the interaction non-rejection is a DETECTABILITY (underpowered) null.

MDE_delta@80=0.3347 is 2.10x the direct tail effect |beta_direct|=0.1595: the design could only have detected an amplification larger than the direct effect, so the interaction non-rejection is a DETECTABILITY (underpowered) null.

MDE_delta@80=0.6079 is 2.52x the direct tail effect |beta_direct|=0.2411: the design could only have detected an amplification larger than the direct effect, so the interaction non-rejection is a DETECTABILITY (underpowered) null.

MDE_delta@80=0.6348 is 2.98x the direct tail effect |beta_direct|=0.2131: the design could only have detected an amplification larger than the direct effect, so the interaction non-rejection is a DETECTABILITY (underpowered) null.

inputs.note: Canonical n_boot=1000 interaction CIs are already on disk; smoke and canonical read the SAME CIs — only the reported horizon set differs.
